{"problem_standardized_math":"Let $(x,\\lambda,s)$ be a point with $(x,s)>0$. Consider the trajectory $\\mathcal{H}$ defined, for $\\tau\\in[0,1]$, by\n\\[\nF\\big(\\hat{x}(\\tau),\\hat{\\lambda}(\\tau),\\hat{s}(\\tau)\\big)\n=\n\\begin{bmatrix}\n(1-\\tau)(A^T\\lambda+s-c)\\\\\n(1-\\tau)(Ax-b)\\\\\n(1-\\tau)XSe\n\\end{bmatrix},\n\\qquad\n(\\hat{x}(\\tau),\\hat{s}(\\tau))\\ge 0,\n\\]\nwhere\n\\[\n\\big(\\hat{x}(0),\\hat{\\lambda}(0),\\hat{s}(0)\\big)=(x,\\lambda,s),\n\\]\nand the limit of $\\big(\\hat{x}(\\tau),\\hat{\\lambda}(\\tau),\\hat{s}(\\tau)\\big)$ as $\\tau\\uparrow 1$ lies in the primal-dual solution set of the linear program. Find equations for the first, second, and third derivatives of $\\mathcal{H}$ with respect to $\\tau$ at $\\tau=0$. Hence, write down a Taylor series approximation to $\\mathcal{H}$ near $(x,\\lambda,s)$."}
